title: Tefal Ingenio Cook Eat Induction Non-Stick 5 Piece Set $89 @ Big W
body:
Deal: Tefal Ingenio Cook Eat Induction Non-Stick 5 Piece Set $89 @ Big W

[huu] Look like only in stock at warringah mall, no delivery
[JerryB] Says free delivery for me in Launnie. Available from Rosny Park, Glenorchy and Kingston stores.
[darkkito] not the biggest fan of this one, the handle seems to fail in about a year and becomes unuseable after as it doesnt clamp on properly.
[mac2403] Had ours for 8 years and they still work well. One did fail cos it cracked from heat but tefal replaced it under warranty. They have have long 10 year warranty so you should request a replacement.
[Upolo] Do you go via the retailer or the Tefal directly for that?
I can’t imagine BIGW staff being interested in taking a pot back years later.
[mac2403] Tefal directly, the sent a new handle out.
[braddsey] Probably great for caravan/campervan or tiny kitchen. Can’t imagine why you would want it otherwise.
[Justin-aus] We used to use 4 drawers for our pans, but replaced with a larger set of these (think there’s 12) and it only takes up half a drawer now. We don’t have a tiny kitchen, but much prefer the better use of space.
[Justin-aus] Had ours several years and no issues. Only annoying thing is they don’t come with lids, so have to buy those separately
[Fabio08] Are microplastics a risk with this or is that just crap?
[bargaino] There may yet prove to be something in the microplastics panic, but teflon cookware is safe, when used correctly. ie no super-hot asian-style frying.
The legit concern is with the chemicls used in their manufacture, not the finished product.
[gakko] Is the concern you refer to small chain pfas?
[ssfps] With the history of industry suppression of known health and environmental concerns, it's reasonable to assume any health risks of PTFE and the surrounding chemicals would not be championed by industry or regulators.
[Justin-aus] Side note, but worth watching Dark Waters movie if anyone hasn’t seen it. For the dangers of teflon manufacturing and contamination controversy.
[rhino86] If you are worried about plastic intake, look at all the food packaging…. its a plastic wonderland in coles and woolies.
[Xizor] Worse yet, food storage containers, tea bags, any black plastic exposed to food and heat is especially bad…. even our water supplies micro and nano plastics are continuously increasing.
Recently stated going down this rabbit hole out of chance then curiosity. Its alarmimg as hell what the leading research indicates how it damages and disrupts at a cellular level. But the issue is so enormous its basically impossible to tackle at a societal level.
[Jackson] The black plasticis no worse, someone screwed up putting a decimal point k. The wrong spot during a calculation, this was widely reported after the initial reports of black plastic being 10x worse. Stick to wood and silicon
This induction set looks like a decent set of cookware ruined by Teflon coating. Saying "of its used in the right way" is like saying "if you are a perfect cook 100% of the time". No one is of course, and these pans don't usually last more than a year or two for most people before the coating turns to shit. In these cases, where do ou think the stuff is going? In your food, down your sink into waterways, in a landfill. Just say no to this and hopefully companies will stop making it. There's way better options anyway
[NigelTufnel] These are alright. Nice option for those with no storage space though, but the lids for the pots are utter crap.
Edit: actually looks like this doesn't come with the lids. You're not missing much.
[railspider] Got these last time they were on sale, really happy with them. Underrated feature with the clip off handle is it's easier to fit all the pans in the dishwasher.
[mikeleemy] quick note. only 2 items are uncoated..the rest are teflon/non stick coated.
[bcYield] Yeah the pots are good while the pans aren't really.
[bcYield] $10 cheaper than last time. Pots are great. Pans are kinda sticky and hard to clean. One handle to be shared. Plastic lids. Good at this price and space saving.
If you are lazy enough, cook and eat in the pot straight. Less cleaning.
Edit: My bad. Last time was 8 pieces including 2 pans and 2 plastic lids. Worked out cheaper.
[GunnerMcDagget] I have been using a whole bunch of these for about 2 years now. I find them great with the exception that the handle gets gunked up pretty easily and it's hard to clean. Used to hand wash it, eventually just started throwing it in the dishwasher figuring that it would die quickly but after 6 months of dishwasher use, they're still going strong.
Plus you can always use an oven mit/dishrag if you don't want to use the handles. Not perfect but it works.
[yannyrjl] I bought the 8 piece version last time from Big W for $99, mainly used for camping trips, although the pots get used at home when everything is occupied.
As others have mentioned, the pots are pretty good, but the non-stick pans don't hold up very well, even after first few uses, they already lose some of the "non-stick" property, and we have them on medium heat and used very carefully. The handle is ok, had no issues with it. I wish the pans are stainless steel, the it would be perfect.
For the special price, it's ok, if mainly just for the stackable pots the non-stick that will prob. last 6 mths or so if used regularly.
[viper8548] do you ever dump it in cold water right after cooking? i find that’s the biggest cause of non stick degrading
